result:
All three deliverables are complete and verified. Final status against the spec:

## T2 complete

**Routing recap:** compact, well-specified task — kept the estimand judgment and integration with the lead, did the run/verify loop directly (no reflexive same-model delegation), and reserved the Codex blind check for ambiguity that empirical verification resolved instead. No Workflow, since there was no parallel structure to fan out. Used **zero of the 3 available delegations** and **one revision cycle**, both within budget.

**Deliverables** (all in `runs/opus/t2/`):

1. **`script.R`** — self-contained; Okabe-Ito palette + `theme_amce` declared at top; `set.seed(1234)` before estimation. Uses `projoint(out, .structure = "profile_level", .estimand = "amce")` and reports the **measurement-error–corrected AMCE** — projoint's own estimator — with SEs clustered on respondent by construction.
2. **`figures/amce-dotwhisker.png`** — 320 dpi (2560×2880). Faceted/grouped by attribute, levels in semantic order within each block, reference levels as open circles fixed at 0 with no interval, 95% CI whiskers, no in-plot title.
3. **`report.md`** — 214-word results paragraph; figure caption sits under the figure reference.

**Verified numbers** (fresh run matches report): violent crime −25.1, 75-min commute −23.7, 40%-income rent −19.8, small town +15.8, mixed suburb +14.6, schools 9-vs-5 +11.6; τ = 0.172 → correction ×1.525.

**Revision made:** the uncertainty sentence originally claimed "most mid-scale contrasts" were imprecise — the audit showed **11 of 17 contrasts are significant** (everything ≥ ~11.5 pp), so I corrected it to state that intervals exclude zero down to ~11 points, with only the racial-composition, partisan-vote, and smallest amenity contrasts indistinguishable from zero. Also swapped the deprecated `geom_errorbarh()` for `geom_errorbar(orientation = "y")`, eliminating the ggplot2 4.0 warning (figure appearance unchanged).

**Remaining warnings are expected:** the three `CR2 → stata/HC1` fallback notices are projoint's own documented non-PSD handling, flagged as such in the script header — not errors.
